name: lhc-olympics-2020

env_axis.name: blind-injection
env_axis.shift_mechanism: RnD set has known signal+background; 3 black-box datasets have unknown injected anomalies — simulates MC-to-data gap

ood_protocol.name: lhc-olympics-blind-bb
ood_protocol.ref: Kasieczka et al. 2021 RPP (LHC Olympics report)

facts: 1M background + 1M signal R&D events + 3 black-box datasets with unknown injected anomalies.
hypotheses: Black-box anomaly scores do not correlate with R&D training: the blind injection succeeds in being method-invariant.
Anomaly detection does not generalize across the 3 black boxes (BB1→BB2, BB3); anomaly discovery is dataset-specific.
intuitions: LHC Olympics retrospective papers from 2020-2024 likely exist.
ATLAS/CMS anomaly detection on actual Run 2/3 physical data probably exists.
unknowns: MC-to-data shift in HEP is compound — parton showers + hadronization + detector response + pileup — but the black boxes don't decompose, and there is no established label for a 'compound shift mechanism'.
Detector-side and physics-side anomalies share data signature with categorically different generation processes.
epistemic_state.boundary: Simulated data; C=2 is event-level only, not within-particle.

representational_commitments: BB1/BB2/BB3 specific anomaly types are the organizers' choice
materialization_status: BB1.h5 materialized (2.1 GB). BB2/BB3 (~6 GB total) + R&D set deferred — same Zenodo /content URL pattern.
